rule: Cursor orbits canvas on a circle. SELECT stamps cursor color onto canvas section aligned with cursor position. 12=top, 6=bottom, 3=right, 9=left, 45deg=diagonals.
meta: Level 1: canvas starts black, target is black-top + white-bottom. Cursor starts white at 12. Navigate to 6 o'clock (RIGHT,DOWN,DOWN,LEFT), SELECT. 5 steps.

rule: Palette shows target pattern. Canvas starts partially matching target. Only stamp what's MISSING. 45deg positions paint diagonal stripes. Stamps overwrite — order matters.
meta: Level 2: target is white-top + black-bottom + olive-diagonal-bottom-right. Canvas already has black bottom. Stamp white from 12 (1 step), then olive from ~4 o'clock (click indicator + rotate + SELECT). Efficiency = see delta between current state and target.

mechanics: basic navigation, rotation pickup, goal config matching
notes: Simple path: left to rot pickup, up to goal
2 refills available, dec=2 so budget management matters
Conveyors teleport player across map. Key discovery: arrows push player to nearest wall/goal boundary
Heavy conveyor usage. No mobile mods. Shape cycling requires multiple pickups (s=4→5 needs +1)

mechanics: mobile rotation modifier (period 4), conveyors, static shape+color
notes: First mobile modifier. Modifier walks along y=35 track. Must time arrival to intercept. Period affects BFS state space via time dimension

mechanics: 2 goals, 3 mobile modifiers (all period 8), 2 conveyors, 3 refills
notes: Hardest level. 2 goals needing different configs. 73M theoretical state space. Distance pruning essential to solve within 15M BFS states. Goal-blocking bug: collected goals must not block movement

mechanics: mobile rotation modifier (period 10), static shape+color, 3 conveyors, 6 refills
notes: Large map with many refills. Mobile rot mod traverses vertical track at x=54

rule: Purple stick: select farthest end from target, place in clear space, then use midpoint formula placed=2*target-other_end for exact landing.
meta: L1 is tutorial — single stick compass. Ball = midpoint of 2 endpoints.

rule: Two compasses: purple stick + orange tripod. Solve stick first (faster convergence). Tripod: ball = centroid of 3 ends, moves by displacement/3. Route around obstacles — ball jumps them, only landing matters.
meta: Obstacles block endpoint placement AND ball landing. But ball can fly over during animation.

rule: Green quadpod (4 legs) + purple stick. S-curve obstacle wraps center. Route quadpod endpoints RIGHT first (x>48), then DOWN through right-side gap in obstacle, then toward target. LEFT side has no safe passage.
meta: Quadpod centroid moves by displacement/4. S-curve upper arm spans full width at y=22-28. Must route through RIGHT-side gap.

rule: 3 compasses: purple+orange+green stick, red+blue stick, yellow+green tripod. Large center obstacle. Solve red+blue first (4 steps), purple second (6 steps), tripod last. Route tripod BELOW obstacle then UP on right side.
meta: Match balls to targets by shared color composition. Multicolored balls go to multicolored targets.

rule: Paradigm shift: white compass balls collect colors by landing on stationary colored balls. Ball absorbs color (visible as pixel change). Must collect ALL colors matching a multicolored target before delivering. White ball is a shopping cart.
meta: L5 changes the mechanic completely. Stationary colored balls + colored target rings. Green+yellow target needs both green AND yellow collected first.

rule: connector between rows = parent-child. Parent slot (top3) gets LAST indicator. Children fill expansion.
meta: dp spotted the connector as structural cue

rule: multiple expansion groups. Each parent gets last indicator of its child group.
meta: two sub-groups with different border colors

rule: parent slot gets the indicator that MATCHES THE BORDER COLOR of its expansion box
meta: requires correct color perception. Andy's color fix was essential.

rule: border color can occupy MULTIPLE parent slots. Count of border-color palette items = parent slot count.
meta: two blues in palette = two blue parent slots

rule: SWAP mechanic (not pick-place). Container box gets child identities in INDICATOR READING ORDER. Each child box gets its content pair.
meta: paradigm shift: pick-place → swap. dp: indicator order determines container contents.

rule: each box = [own_color, CHILD_BOX_COLOR, own_color]. Palindrome indicators encode nesting depth.
meta: russian nesting dolls. center of palindrome = innermost leaf.

rule: each box gets its color group + OTHER box's identity in last position. Mutual acknowledgment.

description: L6 solved via 4-play waypoint chaining. Checkered squares are WAYPOINTS — block docks and saves position when landing on one. Next play starts from saved position. Path: (33,28)→(53,28)→(49,20)→(53,4)→(37,8).
L7: 4-play waypoint path dodging portal kill zone at y=16. Route: (41,28)→(53,24)→(53,8)→(33,12)→(41,8). Portal collision uses inclusive bounds — adjacent sprites count as colliding.
L1: 3 clicks on one brown button. Simplest level — one goal, two buttons, direct path.
L2: forward button depletes after 3 clicks. Use enabler (no visible effect) to refill, then forward again. Pattern: 3F, E, F, E, F.
L3: 3 goals, 8 brown buttons. Some buttons move 2 goals simultaneously. Enabler chain is 3 layers deep. Move goals in priority order.
L4: FIRST LEVEL REQUIRING BLUE BUTTONS. Brown clicks arrange walls until blue turns olive, then click blue for animation. Two blue activations needed.
L5: Most complex. 5 blue-button activations across all 3 blue buttons. 2 goals need structural swaps via blue animations.
L6: 2 blue-button activations. Setup pattern: brown arrange, blue swap, brown forward, blue swap, brown finish.
L7: 3 goals, 4 blue-button activations including 2 back-to-back. Grid is smaller (48x48) with display offset.
